content.text: Self-Hosted K8s Homelab Server

A **production-grade self-hosted homelab server** running a full Kubernetes cluster on consumer hardware — serving real workloads 24/7. From AI inference to workflow automation to databases, everything runs on-premise with zero cloud dependency and a **₹10,000/month cost saving** over equivalent AWS infrastructure.

content.title: Live Services
content.text: **K8s API** - k8s-api.vpjoshi.in · **Public Monitoring** - stats.vpjoshi.in · **Grafana** - grafana.vpjoshi.in · All exposed securely via Cloudflare Tunnel — no open ports, no public IP.
Hardware Specs

items: **Network** - Home ISP + Cloudflare Tunnel for public exposure

content.title: AWS Equivalent
content.text: This hardware is roughly equivalent to an **AWS m5.large** (2 vCPU, 8GB RAM) — which costs ~₹10,000/month. The homelab runs the same workloads at near-zero marginal cost after the one-time hardware purchase.
Cost Savings

content.title: Actual Cost
content.text: Running everything self-hosted costs roughly **₹500-800/month** in electricity — saving **₹10,000+ per month** compared to equivalent cloud services. Hardware paid for itself in under 2 months.
System Architecture

content.title: Cloudflare Tunnel
content.text: No ports are open on the home router. **Cloudflare Tunnel** creates a secure outbound-only connection from the server to Cloudflare's edge — all traffic is proxied through Cloudflare with DDoS protection and TLS termination included for free.
Kubernetes Cluster (k3s)

The server runs a **k3s** (lightweight Kubernetes) cluster — the full K8s API with a fraction of the resource overhead. Every service is deployed as a K8s workload with proper resource limits, health checks, and rolling update support.

content.code:
# Cluster access
export KUBECONFIG=~/.kube/config
kubectl get nodes
# NAME         STATUS   ROLES                  AGE
# homelab      Ready    control-plane,master   90d

kubectl get pods -A
# NAMESPACE    NAME                        READY   STATUS
# default      n8n-xxx                     1/1     Running
# default      llama-xxx                   1/1     Running
# default      pocketbase-xxx              1/1     Running
# default      postgres-xxx                1/1     Running
# monitoring   prometheus-xxx              1/1     Running
# monitoring   grafana-xxx                 1/1     Running
# monitoring   stats-xxx                   1/1     Running

content.title: Why k3s?
content.text: Full K8s on 8GB RAM would leave little headroom for actual workloads. **k3s** uses ~500MB RAM for the control plane vs ~2GB for vanilla K8s — giving the workloads the memory they need.
CI/CD Pipeline

All services are deployed via **GitHub Actions** with a commit-message-based trigger. Only commits containing `[prod]` trigger a build and rolling deploy to the k3s cluster — preventing accidental deploys during development.

content.code:
push to main
    │
    ├── commit contains [prod]?
    │       │
    │       yes → build Docker image
    │             → push to container registry
    │             → kubectl rollout restart on k3s
    │
    └── no [prod] → lint + type-check only

# Trigger a production deploy
git commit -m "feat: update n8n workflow config [prod]"
git push

content.title: Zero Downtime
content.text: K8s rolling updates ensure **zero downtime deploys** — new pods start before old ones terminate. Combined with health checks, a bad deploy is automatically rolled back if the new pod fails to start.
Services Running on the Cluster
n8n - Workflow Automation

**n8n** is a self-hosted workflow automation tool — equivalent to Zapier or Make. Runs as a K8s deployment, persists workflows to Postgres, and handles automated pipelines across APIs, databases, and webhooks.
LLaMA 3.2 - Local AI Inference
**Meta LLaMA 3.2** runs entirely on-premise via Ollama. No API costs, no data leaving the server. Used for local AI-powered tasks across projects — classification, summarization, and chat.
PostgreSQL - Primary Database
A **PostgreSQL** instance runs as a StatefulSet with persistent volume storage. Shared across services — n8n, PocketBase, and project-specific databases all connect to the same cluster.
PocketBase - Backend-as-a-Service

**PocketBase** provides auth, realtime, file storage, and a REST API out of the box. Used as a lightweight backend for internal projects and prototypes — deployed in under a minute as a single binary in a container.

content.title: PocketBase vs Firebase
content.text: At ~10K MAU, Firebase costs ₹3,000-6,000/month. PocketBase on the homelab costs ₹0/month extra — it just runs as another pod. Estimated savings of **₹400-780/year** per project using it.
Monitoring Stack
Two monitoring layers run on the cluster — one for internal use and one for public visibility.
Grafana + Prometheus (Internal)

**Prometheus** scrapes `node_exporter` and `scaphandre` every 10 seconds for full hardware metrics. **Grafana** provides rich dashboards — CPU, RAM, disk I/O, network, power draw, and K8s pod health — accessible at `grafana.vpjoshi.in` (auth protected).
stats.vpjoshi.in (Public)

A custom **Next.js dashboard** that queries Prometheus server-side and renders a public-facing metrics page. Grafana dashboards require login — this exposes live server stats to anyone at `stats.vpjoshi.in` with no auth wall.

items: **CPU load, temperature, power draw** - live and averaged over 1hr
**Estimated monthly electricity cost** - calculated from live wattage × ₹6/unit
**Network I/O** - in/out KB/s over the last hour

content.title: Power Monitoring
content.text: **scaphandre** measures actual host power draw in microwatts. Combined with the local electricity rate (₹6/unit), the dashboard shows a **live estimated monthly electricity bill** — useful for validating the cost savings vs cloud.
stats.vpjoshi.in - Public Monitoring Dashboard

A custom **Next.js 15** dashboard built because Grafana dashboards aren't publicly shareable. It queries Prometheus server-side via API routes and renders live server metrics at **stats.vpjoshi.in** — no login, no auth wall, open to anyone.

content.caption: Live public dashboard — CPU, RAM, power draw, uptime, and network I/O updated every 10 seconds.

content.text: What's on Display

items: **CPU load, temperature, power draw** - live and 1hr average
**Network I/O** - in/out KB/s over last hour

content.title: Built with Next.js + SWR
content.text: Refresh interval is configurable from the UI (10s → 5m). `PROMETHEUS_URL` never leaves the server — browser only talks to `/api/*` routes on the same origin.
Full Technical Stack
Orchestration
Services

items: **Ollama + LLaMA 3.2** - Local AI inference, zero API cost.
**PocketBase** - Lightweight BaaS for auth, realtime, and file storage.

content.text: Monitoring

items: **node_exporter** - CPU, RAM, disk, network hardware metrics.

content.text: Networking & DevOps

items: **Cloudflare Tunnel** - Secure public exposure with no open ports.
**K8s Ingress** - Internal routing to services via subdomains.
**GitHub Actions** - CI/CD with `[prod]` commit-message deploy trigger.
**TLS** - Automatic HTTPS via Cloudflare for all public subdomains.

content.text: Networking Setup

All services are exposed publicly via **Cloudflare Tunnel** — no port forwarding required on the home router. The tunnel creates a secure outbound-only connection, and Cloudflare's ingress routes subdomains to the correct K8s services.

content.code:
# cloudflare tunnel config (simplified)
ingress:
  - hostname: k8s-api.vpjoshi.in
    service: https://localhost:6443
  - hostname: stats.vpjoshi.in
    service: http://stats-svc:3000
  - hostname: grafana.vpjoshi.in
    service: http://grafana-svc:3000
  - hostname: n8n.vpjoshi.in
    service: http://n8n-svc:5678
  - service: http_status:404

content.title: Security Note
content.text: Grafana and n8n are protected with **application-level auth**. The K8s API is secured with kubeconfig certificates. Only `stats.vpjoshi.in` is fully public — it only reads from Prometheus and exposes no write surface.
